name: TV Shows
description: Sitcoms, dramas, iconic characters, and theme songs

questions.q: 'Where Everybody Knows Your Name' is the theme song for which classic Boston-set sitcom?

source.notes: Aired 1982-1993. Written by Gary Portnoy and Judy Hart Angelo.

questions.q: 'Thank You for Being a Friend' is the theme song for which beloved sitcom about four older women?

source.notes: Original song by Andrew Gold (1978). The show version was a cover by Cynthia Fee.

questions.q: Who performed the theme song to the 1990s sitcom 'The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air'?

source.notes: Also played the lead character. Music by DJ Jazzy Jeff.

questions.q: What catchphrase does Homer Simpson utter every time something goes wrong?

source.notes: Added to the Oxford English Dictionary in 2001.

questions.q: On 'Friends,' which character is famous for the pickup line 'How you doin'?'

source.notes: Joey Tribbiani, played by Matt LeBlanc.

questions.q: Which 'Star Trek' character is known for the phrase 'Live long and prosper'?

source.notes: Accompanied by the Vulcan salute. Leonard Nimoy invented the salute based on a Jewish blessing.

questions.q: Which 'Big Bang Theory' character catches people with his signature exclamation 'Bazinga!'?

source.notes: Played by Jim Parsons. A species of jellyfish was named Bazinga rieki after him.

questions.q: In the TV series 'Lost,' what was the flight number of the plane that crashed on the mysterious island?

source.notes: Ran from 2004 to 2010 on ABC. Show creators: J.J. Abrams, Damon Lindelof, Jeffrey Lieber.

questions.q: On the game show 'Jeopardy!', contestants must phrase their responses in what unique format?

source.notes: Hosted by Art Fleming (1964-75), Alex Trebek (1984-2020), and various hosts since.

questions.q: The Netflix series 'Narcos' chronicles the rise and fall of which infamous drug lord?

source.notes: Escobar played by Wagner Moura in seasons 1-2. Based on real DEA investigations.

questions.q: What is the name of the fictional Colorado town where 'South Park' is set?

source.notes: Main characters: Stan, Kyle, Eric Cartman, and Kenny McCormick.

questions.q: Which actress stars as Eleanor Shellstrop in the comedy 'The Good Place'?

source.notes: Co-stars Ted Danson. Created by Michael Schur. Aired 2016-2020.

questions.q: In the Netflix series 'House of Cards,' what is the name of the scheming U.S. politician at its center?

source.notes: Played by Kevin Spacey until his removal in 2017. Based on 1990 British series.

questions.q: The drama 'Ozark' follows money-laundering financial planner Marty Byrde to which U.S. region?

source.notes: Located in Missouri. Starred Jason Bateman and Laura Linney. 2017-2022.

questions.q: In 'The Office' (US), what is Michael Scott's job title at Dunder Mifflin?

source.original_a: Regional Manager of the Scranton branch
source.notes: Scranton, Pennsylvania branch. Played by Steve Carell 2005-2011.

questions.q: The Netflix series 'Orange Is the New Black' begins with which character's imprisonment?

source.notes: Based on Piper Kerman's 2010 memoir. Aired 2013-2019.

questions.q: Dr. Teeth and the Electric Mayhem is the house band from which puppet-based franchise?

source.notes: Includes Animal, Janice, Zoot, and Floyd Pepper. Created by Jim Henson.

questions.q: The New Zealand comedy duo featured in the HBO show 'Flight of the Conchords' come from which country?

source.notes: Jemaine Clement and Bret McKenzie. Self-described 'fourth most popular folk parody duo in New Zealand.'

questions.q: In the original 'The Apprentice,' which host famously told contestants 'You're fired!'?

source.notes: Hosted 2004-2015. Arnold Schwarzenegger took over briefly in 2017.

questions.q: In 'The OC,' the wealthy family that takes in troubled teen Ryan Atwood is the what?

source.notes: Sandy, Kirsten, and Seth Cohen. Set in Newport Beach, California.

questions.q: In 'Cops,' which reggae song serves as the long-running show's iconic theme?

source.notes: By Inner Circle, released 1987. Often paired with the line 'Whatcha gonna do when they come for you?'

questions.q: Who portrayed the young Alex P. Keaton on the 1980s sitcom 'Family Ties'?

source.notes: Ran 1982-1989. Launched Fox's film career including 'Back to the Future.'
